items.headline: Italian Open leaders side with players on prize money issue and aim to become a 5th Grand Slam
items.description:
ROME (AP) — Italian Open organizers are supporting tennis players who are urging a boycott unless the Grand Slam tournaments improve their prize money.
Angelo Binaghi, the president of the Italian Tennis and Padel Federation, is taking advantage of the move as part of a campaign to turn their event into a fifth Grand Slam.

items.headline: Scores arrested in Paris after PSG reach Champions League final
items.description: Police arrested 127 people while 11 people were injured, including one seriously, during incidents in the French capital after Paris Saint-Germain reached the Champions League final, Interior...
